item.title: Trump talks up trade deals with China, but experts see no big wins for U.S.
item.description: President Trump's trip to China could bolster economic relations, but failed to deliver a breakthrough deal, some trade and energy experts said.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: 2003 Space Shuttle Columbia disaster
item.description: On Feb. 1, 2003, the Space Shuttle Columbia disintegrated upon reentering Earth's atmosphere, killing all seven crew members on board. Major Garrett introduces CBS News Radio's coverage from that day.

item.title: Iraqi man charged with planning U.S. terror attack on synagogue, prosecutors say
item.description: An Iraqi national was arrested and charged with planning to carry out several terror attacks in the U.S., including at a New York City synagogue and other prominent Jewish centers in Los Angeles and Scottsdale, Arizona. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: Michigan Democratic Senate candidate Mallory McMorrow calls GOP rival a "formidable competitor"
item.description: The Democratic primary for the Michigan Senate race is heating up with less than three months to go before the election. Candidate and state Sen. Mallory McMorrow joins "The Takeout" to make her case for the upper chamber seat.

item.title: Colorado governor commutes Trump ally Tina Peters' prison sentence
item.description: Colorado's governor on Friday announced he is commuting the sentence of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters.

item.title: Explosion at Maine lumber mill leaves at least 10 injured
item.description: Firefighters responded to a fire and explosion at 10 a.m. at Robbins Lumber in Searsmont, Maine, about 95 miles from Portland.

item.title: Betting on War | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: More than $1 billion has been wagered on prediction markets this year on military decisions and outcomes. This Sunday, 60 Minutes investigates suspiciously timed bets with information that appears too specific for civilian outsiders.

item.title: Boeing says China committed to buy at least 200 aircraft after Trump-Xi summit
item.description: President Trump is heading back to Washington, D.C., after his Beijing summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. The president touted "fantastic trade deals" made between the U.S. and China. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has the details.

item.title: Breaking down Supreme Court decision preserving mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday issued a decision that will allow the abortion pill mifepristone to continue being sent through the mail for now. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks down the ruling.

item.title: Prominent N.Y. synagogue targeted in alleged terror plot, prosecutors say
item.description: An Iraqi national allegedly plotted to carry out terror attacks in the U.S., including at a prominent synagogue in New York, prosecutors said Friday.

item.title: Africa CDC confirms new Ebola outbreak in Congo
item.description: The Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has confirmed a new deadly Ebola outbreak in Congo that has claimed the lives of at least 65 people. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins with analysis.

item.title: Rep. Ro Khanna says he's "concerned that Trump may be compromising" U.S. assurances to Taiwan
item.description: President Trump wrapped up a two-day summit in China with future arms sales to Taiwan in some doubt and scant details on future Chinese purchases of American jets and soybeans. Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna of California joins "The Takeout" with his reaction to the trip.

item.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe meets with Cuban officials in Havana
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with senior Cuban officials in Havana on Thursday, following months of President Trump flirting with taking military action against the island nation. CBS News chief foreign affairs correspondent and "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan reports.

item.title: Lawmakers press Trump for military support for Taiwan after Xi summit
item.description: President Trump told reporters he made "no commitment either way" to China's Xi Jinping regarding U.S. weapons sales to Taiwan.

item.title: Iraqi national planned to bomb Manhattan synagogue, sources say
item.description: Federal prosecutors charged an Iraqi national on Friday with planning several terrorist attacks in the U.S. as retribution for the Iran war. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.

item.title: Multiple wildfires burning in Texas Panhandle
item.description: Wildfires are spreading in the Texas Panhandle, forcing evacuations. CBS News correspondent Jason Allen has the latest.

item.title: U.S. reups $6M reward for American missing in Afghanistan for 12 years
item.description: Paul Edwin Overby Jr. vanished in May 2014 while researching a book in Khost province, Afghanistan, the FBI said.

item.title: Sen. Duckworth on ICE's release of soldier's wife, and her concerns about airline staffing
item.description: The wife of a U.S. Army soldier is now out of Immigration and Customs Enforcement custody after a monthlong detainment. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez spoke with her husband, Sgt. 1st Class Jose Serrano, and Sen. Tammy Duckworth of Illinois joins CBS News to discuss the case. Duckworth also explains why she's calling on the FAA to study the impact of airlines' flight attendant staffing.

item.title: ICE releases wife of U.S. Army soldier after monthlong detention
item.description: ICE has released the wife of an active-duty U.S. soldier after a month in detention, her husband told CBS News.

item.title: 2 senators call on FAA to study impact of reduced flight attendant staffing
item.description: Sens. Tammy Duckworth​ of Illinois and Tammy Baldwin of Wisconsin say their concern is there may be more emergency exit doors than flight attendants in the event of an evacuation.

item.title: Which college majors offer the best long-term return on investment?
item.description: College grads outearn people without a degree within 15 years, even after paying for tuition, study finds.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen drops reelection bid in wake of Tennessee redistricting
item.description: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen of Tennessee ended his reelection campaign on Friday. The decision comes after the state's GOP lawmakers voted to split up the Memphis congressional district he's represented for almost 20 years.

item.title: The Knowledge | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: London's iconic black cabs are about to compete with AI-powered, autonomous taxis. But the city's cabbies aren't ready to hand over their keys. Anderson Cooper reports, Sunday on 60 Minutes.

item.title: Cuba's biggest threat to U.S. is collapse, says former defense secretary
item.description: In an interview with "Face the Nation," Gates said another mass exodus from Cuba is the "biggest risk."

item.title: How will Jerome Powell be remembered as he exits as Fed chair?
item.description: As Powell steps down after more than eight years leading the Federal Reserve, economists say he helped steer the U.S. through historic shocks but misread inflation.

item.title: Pirro threatens charges against parents if their teens violate local curfew
item.description: In a move aimed at curbing the growing problem of "teen takeovers," D.C. U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro is threatening to bring charges against parents if their teens violate the local curfew.

item.title: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen drops reelection bid after redistricting
item.description: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen of Tennessee announced Friday that he's retiring from Congress at the end of this term after his district in Memphis was redrawn.

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo kills dozens, Africa's CDC says
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak in a remote province in Congo has caused 65 deaths and 246 suspected cases so far, Africa CDC says.

item.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe makes rare trip to Cuba as island faces energy crisis
item.description: The meeting came as Cuba is contending with a massive power failure to its national energy grid amid U.S. sanctions that have caused an oil and gas shortage crisis.
